name: Grammar assessment — prototype bank
note: 20 hand-picked items (5 each across 4 topics). difficulty is set by hand; discrimination/guess (IRT) come later (Psychometrics v5).

questions.question: I have ___ apple and ___ banana.
questions.explanation: Use 'an' before a vowel sound (apple) and 'a' before a consonant sound (banana).

questions.question: She plays ___ piano very well.
questions.explanation: We use 'the' with musical instruments: play the piano.

questions.question: Can you give me ___ advice?
questions.explanation: 'Advice' is uncountable, so 'a/an' is wrong; 'some' fits.

questions.question: He is ___ honest person.
questions.explanation: 'Honest' begins with a silent 'h' (a vowel sound), so use 'an'.

questions.question: ___ water in this bottle is warm.
questions.explanation: Specific water (the water in this bottle) takes 'the'.

questions.question: ___ you ever ___ to Japan?
questions.explanation: Present perfect for life experience: have + past participle (been).

questions.question: She ___ here since 2019.
questions.explanation: 'Since 2019' with an unfinished period needs present perfect.

questions.question: We ___ each other for ten years.
questions.explanation: Stative verb 'know' uses simple present perfect, not continuous.

questions.question: He ___ his keys, so he can't get in now.
questions.explanation: A past action with a present result takes present perfect.

questions.question: I ___ this film three times already.
questions.explanation: 'Already' with experience up to now takes present perfect; past participle is 'seen'.

questions.question: If it ___ tomorrow, we'll stay home.
questions.explanation: First conditional: present simple in the if-clause, will in the main clause.

questions.question: If I ___ rich, I would travel the world.
questions.explanation: Second conditional (unreal present): if + past, would + base. 'Were' is standard.

questions.question: She would call you if she ___ your number.
questions.explanation: Second conditional: the if-clause uses the past simple ('had').

questions.question: If you ___ harder, you would pass the exam.
questions.explanation: Second conditional pairs past simple with 'would'.

questions.question: If I ___ you, I wouldn't worry about it.
questions.explanation: Fixed advice form: 'If I were you...' (subjunctive).

questions.question: The window ___ by the boys yesterday.
questions.explanation: Past passive: was/were + past participle, with 'by' for the agent.

questions.question: English ___ all over the world.
questions.explanation: Present passive: is/are + past participle (spoken).

questions.question: The new bridge ___ next year.
questions.explanation: Future passive: will be + past participle (built).

questions.question: This song ___ by millions of people so far.
questions.explanation: Present perfect passive: has/have been + past participle (heard).

questions.question: The letters ___ every morning.
questions.explanation: Plural subject (letters) needs 'are' + past participle (delivered).
